name: design-to-code
description: Pixel-perfect Figma to React conversion using coderio. Generates production-ready code (TypeScript, Vite, TailwindCSS V4) with high visual fidelity. Features robust error handling, checkpoint recovery, and streamlined execution via helper script.
capabilities: Fetch designs from Figma API using user-provided token and URL
Generate component structure and hierarchy from Figma frames
Extract props and state data from Figma component instances
Produce production-ready React TypeScript code with TailwindCSS V4
Manage asset downloads and file organization for generated code
author: MigoXLab
securitySummary: All 121 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate Figma-to-React conversion tool that uses network (Figma API), filesystem (read/write project files), and external commands (Node.js scripts) as expected for its purpose. No credential exfiltration, no malicious code, no prompt injection attempts detected.
valueStatement: Transform Figma designs into production-ready React TypeScript components with pixel-perfect accuracy. This skill automates the design-to-code workflow using AI to generate clean, structured code.
userTitle: Convert Figma Designs to React Code
limitations: Requires valid Figma API token and file access permissions
User must manually attach Figma thumbnail for AI visual reference
Does not support complex interactive behaviors or animations
Component generation requires sequential execution of phases

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers who receive design files from designers
useCases.title: Frontend Developer Workflow
useCases.description: Quickly convert Figma mockups into working React components for prototyping or production use.

useCases.targetUser: Startup developers and product teams building MVPs
useCases.title: Rapid Prototyping
useCases.description: Generate boilerplate React code from design files to accelerate MVP development.

useCases.targetUser: Design system engineers maintaining component libraries
useCases.title: Design System Implementation
useCases.description: Extract individual components from Figma design systems into reusable React code.

promptTemplates.title: Simple Figma Fetch
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the design-to-code skill to fetch my Figma design. The Figma file URL is [FIGMA_URL] and my access token is [TOKEN].
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Fetch a Figma design for conversion

promptTemplates.title: Component Structure Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the structure-prompt command, then analyze the attached thumbnail.png to generate the component structure JSON. Save the result to scripts/structure-output.json.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - Generate component hierarchy from design

promptTemplates.title: Props Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Run list-components to see available components. Then for each component, run props-prompt [COMPONENT_NAME], analyze the thumbnail, and save the props to scripts/[COMPONENT_NAME]-props.json.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - Extract data props from Figma components

promptTemplates.title: Full Design to Code Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute the full design-to-code pipeline: 1) fetch-figma with my token, 2) generate structure and process, 3) extract props for all components, 4) generate code for each task using code-prompt and save-code.

bestPractices: Always attach the Figma thumbnail.png when requesting AI to analyze or generate code
Execute phases in sequential order: Setup → Protocol → Code
Verify checkpoint files exist (processed.json, protocol.json) before proceeding to next phase
antiPatterns: Skipping the thumbnail attachment during AI analysis steps
Running code generation before props extraction is complete
Using invalid or expired Figma API tokens

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a Figma account, a Figma Personal Access Token, and Node.js 18+ installed. The skill uses coderio npm package for Figma operations.

faq.question: How does the Figma token work?
faq.answer: The token is provided by you when running fetch-figma. It is used only to access the specific Figma file you specify. No token is stored by the skill.

faq.question: Can I convert any Figma design?
faq.answer: Most static Figma designs convert well. Complex interactive components, animations, and nested prototypes may require manual adjustments after conversion.

faq.question: What code does this generate?
faq.answer: The skill generates React TypeScript components using Vite project structure and TailwindCSS V4 for styling.

faq.question: Why do I need to attach the thumbnail?
faq.answer: The AI uses the visual thumbnail to understand spacing, layout nuances, and text content that may not be fully captured in the raw Figma JSON data.

faq.question: What happens if a step fails?
faq.answer: The skill provides error messages indicating what went wrong. Common issues include: invalid tokens, missing thumbnail, empty AI responses, or running steps out of order.
